title: Exec not working after update to 3.4.0
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I have always been able to enter a container with the following command, or in general run commands on a running container:

`podman exec -it container_name /bin/bash`

After updating to podman version 3.4.0 on Fedora 34 and without any other relevant changes, I get the following error.

`Error: OCI runtime error: the container '5475adea1a2d7c58d216c064e4c1afe619a2564c59754d419b756ed7927f7d10' is paused.`

The container is certainly running. The status is `Up 4 minutes ago` on `podman ps` and I can access the web server from the exposed port as usual.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run a container in a pod with podman 3.4.0
2. Run the `podman exec` command on the container 
3. Observe the error

**Describe the results you received:**

Error

**Describe the results you expected:**

Run the command in the container

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

- I run the container within a pod
- Only the `podman` related packages (podman, podman-gvproxy, podman-plugins) were updated in the update i.e. no other container related packages. 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.0
API Version:  3.4.0
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Thu Sep 30 14:40:21 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body:
Please look here:
https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/746

Already fixed. You have to update crun to version 1.2.
Closing as this is a crun bug

To make this issue easier to find for others:

We ran into it on Ubuntu 20.04 with podman 3.3.1

podman version
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

People might still have this problem (crun 1.2 ist noch uploaded to ubuntu repo)
Work around is
edit: vim /etc/containers/containers.conf
`# Default OCI runtime  
  runtime = "runc"`

and install runc with apt.
